title: Finding North (Sun and Shadows)
immediate_steps: Find a straight stick about 3 feet long and push it upright into flat ground
Mark the tip of the stick's shadow with a small stone (this is West)
Wait 15-30 minutes for the sun to move
Mark the new position of the shadow's tip with another stone (this is East)
Draw a straight line between the two stones. Stand with the first stone on your left and the second on your right; you are now facing True North
avoid: Do not use a curved stick
Do not move the stick during the process
Do not rely on this near the equator (it is less accurate)

title: Finding North (Stars)
immediate_steps: Locate the Big Dipper constellation (Ursa Major)
Find the two stars forming the outer edge of the Dipper's 'bowl' (the pointer stars)
Draw an imaginary line straight up from those two stars, about five times the distance between them
The star you hit is Polaris (the North Star), which is the tip of the Little Dipper's handle
Face Polaris; you are facing True North
avoid: Do not confuse the Big Dipper with other formations
Do not rely on the position of the moon (it is complex to navigate by)

title: Lost In Forest - Finding Direction
immediate_steps: Observe the position of the sun during daytime to estimate east and west directions
Look for natural landmarks such as rivers, trails, hills and clearings
If carrying a compass, verify direction periodically
avoid: Avoid moving in circles by selecting a fixed distant object as a reference point while walking
